title: Deployment recommendation matrix
acceptanceTarget: Beginner, freelancer, agency, and production team each have a primary recommendation, alternative option, and explicit avoid list.

recommendations.alternative: Local Docker Desktop for learning volume, credentials, and workflow export behavior
avoid: self-hosted VPS with real client credentials before backup and security are understood
AWS, Kubernetes, or queue mode as the first deployment
recommendations.why: The beginner should learn automation semantics before owning hosting, backups, public URLs, secure cookies, and database operations.
recommendations.costRisk: Subscription cost is easier to understand than variable infrastructure cost; the main risk is overbuying before workflows are valuable.
first90Days: Build workflows on n8n Cloud or local Docker
Avoid public self-hosting until backup and restore are practiced

recommendations.primaryRecommendation: Single VPS with Docker Compose, PostgreSQL, Caddy, backup, and patch cadence
recommendations.alternative: Railway, Render, or Fly with managed or external PostgreSQL; Railway template databases require operator-owned backups, disaster recovery, security, and monitoring; n8n Cloud when the client pays for low operations
avoid: usage-priced platform without monthly budget alerts
AWS ECS/RDS or Kubernetes for a small client workflow
single Docker container without PostgreSQL backup
recommendations.why: The freelancer needs a clear client bill, predictable maintenance scope, and a deployment that can be restored without a platform team.
recommendations.costRisk: VPS is mostly fixed but labor-heavy; PaaS is faster but RAM, CPU, storage, egress, and database plan can drift.
first90Days: Use PostgreSQL rather than SQLite for paid work
Add storage and execution pruning alerts

recommendations.primaryRecommendation: Standardized client blueprint using isolated Compose or PaaS instances, managed or external PostgreSQL, backup, patch cadence, and incident notes
recommendations.alternative: n8n Cloud Business or Enterprise for low-operations client delivery
avoid: multiple clients on one unisolated instance
manual one-off deployments without a runbook
usage-priced platform without per-client budget ownership
recommendations.why: The agency optimizes for repeatability, client isolation, handoff, and maintenance margins rather than a single lowest monthly bill.
recommendations.costRisk: The largest risk is hidden labor from many small incidents; per-client budgets, support terms, and standardized restore drills matter more than a tiny compute saving.
first90Days: Define one supported blueprint per client tier
Separate each client database and encryption key
Use Week 17 incident note for every incident

recommendations.primaryRecommendation: AWS or GCP production architecture with managed PostgreSQL, Redis queue, workers, centralized logs, IaC, budgets, and on-call
recommendations.alternative: n8n Cloud Enterprise or mature PaaS with managed database and Redis during transition
avoid: queue mode without centralized logs and DB capacity planning
autoscaling without maximum instance limits and budget alerts
production platform without RPO, RTO, and security review
recommendations.why: The production team has compliance, private networking, IAM, SLO, queue workers, logs, and disaster recovery requirements that justify cloud architecture complexity.
recommendations.costRisk: AWS and GCP cost is a stack total: compute, RDS, Redis, logs, load balancer, NAT, egress, backup, storage, support, and operations labor.
first90Days: Load-test queue workers before production cutover
